title: Get started with tabfm
text: TabFM’s inference code Python package, tabfm drives reticulate. install_tabfm() creates dedicated virtualenv (needs Python >= 3.11 tell can’t find one) installs everything: first time fit model, pretrained weights downloaded Hugging Face Hub cached ~/.cache/huggingface/. may see warning unauthenticated requests; ’s harmless. silence (download faster), put free Hugging Face access token ~/.Renviron HF_TOKEN=hf_.... weights cached, can speed session’s first fit — account needed — adding HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 (load straight cache, skipping Hub’s revalidation requests) JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR=~/.cache/jax (reuse compiled XLA artifacts across sessions) ~/.Renviron. Unset HF_HUB_OFFLINE whenever need download weights don’t yet.
code: install_tabfm()            # JAX backend, CPU # install_tabfm("pytorch") # PyTorch backend, use on a GPU machine

title: Get started with tabfm
text: tabfm() takes data frame predictors response vector, like lm()’s cousins everywhere. Character factor columns handled natively — dummy coding, scaling, imputation required: “Fitting” instant: TabFM learns table -context rather training . loaded model stays memory, can predict many times like without paying load cost . predict() returns class labels default: Use type = "prob" probability matrix, one column per class:

title: Get started with tabfm
text: Pass numeric response task = "regression":
code: fit <- tabfm(mtcars[-1], mtcars$mpg, task = "regression") predict(fit, head(mtcars[-1])) #> [1] 21.00013 21.00013 22.76699 21.38826 18.68293 18.10942

what: When should you reach for TabFM?
title: Get started with tabfm
text: TabFM shines want strong baseline now: preprocessing pipeline, tuning grid, cross-validation loop. particularly good fit small--medium tables fitting bespoke model risks overfitting validation set. right tool : need squeeze accuracy one specific dataset — TabFM zero-shot, nothing tune. Reach tidymodels boosted trees. data doesn’t fit memory alongside model, since every prediction re-reads training table -context. can’t ship Python runtime. Predictions always run Python package.

text: Matt O'Reilly. Author, maintainer.

what: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
title: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
text: goal tabfm make TabFM, Google Research’s zero-shot foundation model tabular data, easy use R. give data frame response; gives classification regression predictions feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, retraining — model predicts unseen tables -context, single forward pass. See announcement blog post details works. tabfm wraps official Python package reticulate, exposes familiar R modelling interface: fitting function plus predict().

title: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
text: can install development version tabfm like : , one time , install underlying Python package dedicated virtualenv:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("mattyoreilly/tabfm") library(tabfm) install_tabfm()            # JAX backend, CPU # install_tabfm("pytorch") # PyTorch backend, CPU/GPU

title: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
text: Fitting instant — TabFM learns table -context rather training — loaded model stays memory, repeated predictions cheap. Character factor columns handled natively; never need dummy-code. Regression works way — pass numeric response task = "regression":
code: library(tabfm)  fit <- tabfm(iris[-5], iris$Species) fit #> TabFM v1.0.0 zero-shot classification model | 4 features  predict(fit, head(iris[-5])) #> [1] "setosa" "setosa" "setosa" "setosa" "setosa" "setosa"  predict(fit, head(iris[-5]), type = "prob") #>         setosa   versicolor    virginica #> [1,] 0.9997242 1.378794e-04 1.378794e-04 #> [2,] 1.0000000 1.902007e-08 1.902007e-08 #> [3,] 1.0000000 1.902007e-08 1.902007e-08 #> [4,] 0.9999967 1.619624e-06 1.619624e-06 #> [5,] 0.9998604 1.378898e-04 1.619404e-06 #> [6,] 0.9997242 1.378794e-04 1.378794e-04 fit <- tabfm(mtcars[-1], mtcars$mpg, task = "regression") predict(fit, head(mtcars[-1])) #> [1] 21.00013 21.00013 22.76699 21.38826 18.68293 18.10942

title: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
text: pretrained weights cached first download, two optional lines ~/.Renviron (Hugging Face account needed) make cold starts noticeably faster: HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 loads weights straight local cache instead revalidating Hugging Face Hub session’s first fit — unauthenticated requests get slowest rate-limit tier, skips wait entirely. Unset whenever need download weights don’t yet. JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR lets new sessions reuse compiled XLA artifacts previous ones instead recompiling model graph. Within session, loaded model cached automatically: first tabfm() call per task pays load cost.

title: R Interface to TabFM, a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data
text: Predictions require working Python installation; install_tabfm() sets everything via reticulate. first tabfm() call downloads pretrained weights Hugging Face Hub (cached afterwards ~/.cache/huggingface/). may see warning unauthenticated requests; ’s harmless, can silence get faster downloads putting free Hugging Face access token ~/.Renviron: HF_TOKEN=hf_... TabFM zero-shot: nothing tune, also way trade compute accuracy specific dataset. need , reach tidymodels.

what: Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
title: Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
text: install_tabfm() installs underlying TabFM Python package dedicated virtualenv via reticulate::py_install(). need run per machine (per backend).

title: Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
text: backend Compute backend: "jax" (default, CPU) "pytorch" (CPU/GPU). envname Name virtualenv install . default, "r-tabfm", discovered automatically reticulate package loads. ... Additional arguments passed reticulate::py_install().

title: Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
text: NULL, invisibly. Called side effect.

title: Install the TabFM Python package — install_tabfm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ install_tabfm()            # JAX backend, CPU install_tabfm("pytorch")   # PyTorch backend, CPU/GPU } # }

what: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
title: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
text: Generate predictions — class labels, class probabilities, numeric values — model fitted tabfm().

title: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
code: # S3 method for class 'tabfm' predict(object, newdata, type = c("response", "prob"), ...)

title: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
text: object "tabfm" object returned tabfm(). newdata data frame exactly columns training data. type return: "response" (default) class labels numeric predictions, "prob" matrix class probabilities (classification ). ... Ignored, compatibility predict() generic.

title: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
text: type = "response", vector one prediction per row newdata: character class labels classification, numeric values regression. type = "prob", numeric matrix one row per row newdata one column per class, named class.

title: Predict from a TabFM model — predict.tabfm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- tabfm(iris[-5], iris$Species)  # class labels predict(fit, head(iris[-5]))  # class probabilities, one column per class predict(fit, head(iris[-5]), type = "prob") } # }

what: tabfm: R Interface to 'TabFM', a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data — tabfm-package
title: tabfm: R Interface to 'TabFM', a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data — tabfm-package
text: thin wrapper around 'TabFM' 'Python' package Google Research (https://github.com/google-research/tabfm). Provides zero-shot classification regression tabular data via -context learning, feature engineering hyperparameter tuning.

title: tabfm: R Interface to 'TabFM', a Zero-Shot Foundation Model for Tabular Data — tabfm-package
text: Maintainer: Matt O'Reilly fermoymatt@gmail.com Authors: Matt O'Reilly fermoymatt@gmail.com

what: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
title: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
text: tabfm() loads pretrained TabFM v1.0.0 weights conditions training data. TabFM learns -context — single forward pass table — feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, retraining. Loading weights slow part, happens per session task/backend combination model reused later fits; repeated calls predict() cheap. Fitted models survive saveRDS()/readRDS(): zero-shot fit holds learned state beyond training data, restored fit re-conditioned stored data automatically first time call predict() new session (also pays session's one-time weights load).

title: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
code: tabfm(   x,   y,   task = c("classification", "regression"),   backend = c("jax", "pytorch") )

title: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
text: x data frame predictors. Character factor columns handled natively TabFM; dummy coding needed. y response: character vector factor classification, numeric vector regression. Must one value per row x. task Type model load: "classification" (default) "regression". backend Compute backend: "jax" "pytorch". Must match backend installed install_tabfm().

title: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
text: model object class "tabfm": list carrying training data (x, y), task, backend, features (training column names), cache holding fitted Python estimator. Use predict() generate predictions .

title: Fit a zero-shot TabFM model — tabfm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- tabfm(iris[-5], iris$Species) fit  predict(fit, head(iris[-5])) predict(fit, head(iris[-5]), type = "prob")  fit <- tabfm(mtcars[-1], mtcars$mpg, task = "regression") predict(fit, head(mtcars[-1])) } # }

title: tabfm 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Fitted models survive saveRDS()/readRDS(): restored fit re-conditions stored training data automatically first predict() new session. tabfm() fits zero-shot classification regression models using Google Research’s TabFM foundation model. predict() method returns class labels, class probabilities (type = "prob"), numeric predictions. install_tabfm() performs one-time setup underlying Python package dedicated virtualenv, requiring Python >= 3.11.
